rows.notes: Large centered page title, clearly written and unambiguous.
Clear centered account heading directly under page title.

Left-side receipt entry is legible as 'From Rotherhithe Trust'. Left three-column numbers read as '364' (pounds) and '2' (shillings); no pence digits visible so pence set to 0. Vertical ruling alignment for these figures is clear.

A second left-hand numeric line under the Library receipts shows the same '364 2' amount with no description. Interpreted as a subtotal/rolled-up figure. The lack of descriptive text and the repetition suggests a total line; the digits are clear but the purpose (label missing) is inferred.
Right-side payment item is clearly readable and the three-column amounts align well: 42 pounds, 11 shillings, 4 pence. All currency columns validate.

Description is clear. The numeric entry adjacent appears as '60' in the pounds column with no shillings/pence shown; interpreted as 60 pounds 0s. There is minor ambiguity because the writer omitted small units, but alignment supports 60 pounds.
Description and amounts clearly legible on right side: 31 pounds, 19 shillings, 4 pence. Column alignment is strong and values validate.
Right-side text and amounts are clear: 229 pounds, 8 shillings, 4 pence. Vertical rules help locate each column precisely.

Bottom-right of Library Account shows a horizontal total line reading '364 0 0' (pounds, shillings, pence). The digits are clear although the arithmetic summation with left-side receipts is inferred from ledger layout.
Centered account heading clearly written and legible.
Left-side receipts under Pension Fund read clearly as 742 pounds, 4 shillings, 3 pence. Text is legible.

Numeric amount (2102 7 6) is clear. The descriptive name immediately to the left is partially faded/ornate; read as 'Sale of Shielington Living' but the proper noun is uncertain (handwriting ambiguous). Chosen spelling approximates the script.
Left-side entry 'From Trustees Funds' is clear; amount reads 200 pounds exactly (no shillings/pence written).
Left-side subtotal for Pension Fund receipts is clearly printed as 3024 pounds, 11 shillings, 9 pence (aligns with the receipts above).

Right-side first payment for Pension Fund reads as a large purchase of consol-like securities. Amount reads 2101 17 7; description is in slightly abbreviated/script form ('Consols' &c) but identifiable. There is moderate ambiguity in the exact wording of the descriptor but the numbers align under the right-side pounds/shillings/pence columns.

A small right-side line reads like an agency/fees item but the numeric columns here are faint and partially obscured; could be a small shilling/pence amount. Rather than risk misreading a small ambiguous figure, amounts are left blank and the description recorded as seen ('Agency &c').

There is a second purchase line (also referring to Consols) showing a round 900 pounds in the right-hand numeric columns. The descriptor is slightly cramped but readable; numeric value is decently clear though ink is lighter than previous lines.
Right-side closing line repeats the ledger total as '3024 11 9' and is well-aligned under the right-hand columns. Clear and validates against the left subtotal.
Centered account heading clearly legible.
Left-side receipt line clearly reads 'Transfer from Composition (General acct)' with numeric 8 pounds, 17 shillings, 6 pence aligned correctly in the three columns.
Left-side 'Rent' entry and 37 9 7 in the £ s d columns are clear.
Dividends line shows 9 pounds, 1 shilling, 1 pence; legible though the pence digit is small and the script slightly compressed.
Left-side beginning balance is written as 97 10 11; digits are clear and align with the columns.
Left-side subtotal visible as 152 18 0; alignment and digits clear. (Shillings 18 is within valid range.)
Right-side closing line for this small account repeats 152 18 0; digits and rule alignment clear.
Centered account heading is clearly written.

Left-side tuition fees numeric reads '4722 14' in the pounds and shillings columns; there is no pence digit shown so pence taken as 0. The handwriting of the descriptor is clear but the small pence column is empty.
This left-side line is legible as '14 15 5' and text matches expected 'Dividends & Interest'.
Left-side total for receipts is clearly printed '4737 9 5'. Good alignment under the receipts column group.

Beginning-balance text is legible, but the pounds figure is ambiguous in the manuscript (looks like '706' or possibly '706?'); chosen reading 706 16 2 is tentative. Ink here is lighter and digits run together.

A left-side summed total under Tuition Fund receipts appears as '4444 5 7' (digits readable though the leading '4's are stylised). This is treated as an intermediate total; handwriting slightly cramped hence moderate confidence.
Right-side payment line is clear and the phrase '(being Fellows)' is legible. Amount 2116 13 4 aligns strongly with the right-hand £ s d columns.
The contracted 'do' (ditto) line for tutors not being fellows is clear and the numeric 277 4 6 is visible and aligns properly.
Descriptor and numeric amount 23 3 6 are clear in the right-hand columns.
There is a short right-side line showing 29 15 5 adjacent to payment descriptions; descriptor is abbreviated/packed but the numeric value is fairly clear.
Right-side entry 'Sub-Rector' is legible; pounds read as 150. No shillings/pence shown so set to 0. Ink for this line is lighter than others but digits are distinct.

Descriptor 'Stationery &c' is readable but the numeric entry is faint and crowded; read as 11 pounds, 1 shilling, 10 pence though the pence digit is close to adjacent strokes. This reading is uncertain due to ink bleed and cramped figures.
Right-side transfer line is clearly written and the numeric '200' is crisp in the pounds column; shillings/pence absent (interpreted as 0).
There is a line for 'Investment' with a round figure that reads as '500' in the pounds column. Ink lighter here; reading is plausible but not as crisp as major totals.
Right-side payments total is clearly printed as 3339 8 7. Alignment under payments column group is strong.

Closing balance for Tuition Fund is present but the pounds figure is ambiguous in the manuscript (looks like '1044' or possibly '3444'); chosen reading '1044 5 7' is tentative. Ink smudge and cramped numerals produced low confidence.
Centered account heading clear and legible.
Left-side receipt 'From Corporate Revenues' with amounts 201 6 8 is clearly readable and well-aligned.
Left-side beginning balance '56 6 8' is clear.
Left-side subtotal for Exhibition Fund reads '257 13 4' clearly and matches the sum of the two receipts above.
Right-side payment line 'Paid to Exhibitions' equals the receipts total (257 13 4) and is clearly written.
A right-side closing line repeats the figure '257 13 4'. The ledger shows this repetition; ink alignment is slightly lighter than the left subtotal, giving moderate confidence.
Centered account heading clearly legible at bottom of page.
Left-side subscription receipt clearly reads '41 8' in pounds and shillings; pence digit absent so recorded as 0. Text 'By Subscriptions' is legible.

There is a short left-side line for a beginning balance; the script suggests a small or zero opening balance but the exact digits are faint. Recorded as zero with low confidence because the field is not clearly written.
Left-side subtotal repeats 41 8 0 and is clearly aligned under the reading-room receipts column.
Right-side 'Sundry Receipts &c' amount reads clearly as 29 5 6; descriptor is legible.
Right-side closing balance for the Reading Room Fund reads clearly as 11 2 6; aligns well with the receipts and subtotal on the left (41 8 0 = 29 5 6 + 11 2 6).
